generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors and unresolved references, not altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize repairs that address type mismatches between assumed and actual types in AGREE expressions.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared AGREE helpers in expressions; avoid references to AADL properties not exposed as ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` keywords with specific syntax.
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE expressions are visible or properly declared within the annex.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to directly translate AADL properties (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) into AGREE expressions without corresponding ports or constants.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or data types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction (component types, implementations, features, connections)
Component type scope and implementation scope rules
AGREE contract placement within AADL scopes
